_doc: Worked example route-table for modelpipe (src/router.mjs). Copy it, set the env vars it names, then start the router and point your Anthropic-format client at it: `modelpipe routes.json` then `export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8787`. The router keys on each request body's `model` and forwards to the FIRST route whose `match` glob matches (so order specific routes before broad ones). Keys are referenced by env-var NAME only — NEVER put a key value in this file. Provider endpoints/auth come from providers.json. ROUTING PROFILES (see docs/profiles.md) replace the old failover/failoverGroups/schedules — run `modelpipe migrate` on an old config.

_route_shape.match: REQUIRED string — glob over the model id; only `*` is a wildcard (e.g. claude-* / deepseek-* / google/*)
_route_shape.base_url: REQUIRED string — the backend origin (optionally with a base path, e.g. /anthropic); the client's /v1/messages path is appended
_route_shape.auth: REQUIRED — EITHER the string "passthrough" (forward the client's own auth header unchanged) OR an object { header, keyEnv, scheme?, fallback? }: the header name, the NAME of the env var holding the key (read at request time, never logged), an OPTIONAL scheme prefix ("Bearer" ⇒ `Authorization: Bearer <key>`), and OPTIONAL fallback:true (forward the client's OWN token when present, inject keyEnv only when it didn't).
_route_shape.billing: OPTIONAL "metered" | "subscription". How the dashboard reports money. "metered" = pay-as-you-go, real per-token $ (DeepSeek, OpenRouter). "subscription" = a flat plan (GLM Coding Plan, an Anthropic subscription) — tokens + a "flat plan" label, never a fabricated cost. DEFAULT: passthrough ⇒ subscription, GLM/z.ai ⇒ subscription, other key-swap ⇒ metered.
_route_shape.accounts: OPTIONAL array of multiple accounts/keys for the SAME model — the router rotates to the next when one hits its limit (429/529/quota/credit). Each is { label, auth, base_url? }. Key/backend-level rotation — the model id is NOT rewritten, and it sits BELOW profiles (a profile step only fires once the whole pool is drained). Cooldown recovery via failoverRecoveryIntervalMs.
_route_shape.strategy: OPTIONAL "failover" (default) | "round-robin" — only with accounts. failover drains account #1 then #2 on a limit; round-robin spreads requests to stretch total quota.
_route_shape.forImages: OPTIONAL true on exactly ONE route — the vision fallback target. An image-bearing request rejected with an image-unsupported 400 is rerouted here.
_route_shape.forImagesModel: REQUIRED on the forImages route — the model id the vision backend expects; on reroute ONLY body.model is rewritten to this.
_route_shape.vision: OPTIONAL boolean, default true. false ⇒ this backend has no vision; an image-bearing request matched here goes STRAIGHT to the forImages target.

_config_shape.dashboard: OPTIONAL boolean, default false — enables /dashboard, /v1/stats, /v1/profiles, and per-model usage tracking. Live state (the active profile pin/offset) persists under ~/.modelpipe/ across restarts.
_config_shape.tokenPrices: OPTIONAL object { modelGlob: { input, output, cacheRead? } } — per-model metered API prices in $ per 1M tokens; keys may use * globs; falls back to the built-in catalog. Editable at runtime and persisted.
_config_shape.profiles: REQUIRED (for routing profiles) object { name: { bind: { aliasGlob: targetModelId } } } — each profile is a NAMED SNAPSHOT of alias→target bindings ('which model goes where'). A client sends a stable alias (e.g. glm-5.2); the ACTIVE profile rewrites it to a concrete target BEFORE route matching. An empty bind = no rewrite (every alias routes to its own backend). First matching alias glob wins. See _profiles_doc and docs/profiles.md.
_config_shape.defaultProfile: OPTIONAL string — the base profile when nothing else selects; defaults to auto.steps[0].profile.
_config_shape.auto: OPTIONAL object { steps, recover?, schedules? } — the automatic selection. steps is the ORDERED chain that steps automatically (best→fallback); membership in steps = automatic, absence = manual-only. See _auto_doc.
_config_shape.compact: OPTIONAL object — context FITTING safety net for a failover downshift to a smaller-window backup, ON BY DEFAULT (see docs/compaction.md). window maps model ids/globs to context size, FIRST MATCH WINS.
_config_shape.concurrency: OPTIONAL object { modelGlob: maxConcurrent } — max SIMULTANEOUS in-flight requests per (provider, model). Some providers cap concurrent requests per subscription/key (e.g. z.ai GLM Coding Plan allows only a few glm-5.2 at once); firing more just earns a 429. Instead, the overflow is held in a FIFO QUEUE until a slot frees, so the client keeps the strong model — it just waits. FIRST MATCH WINS (order specific ids before broad globs: glm-5.2 before glm-*). Unmatched = unlimited (zero overhead). PER ACCOUNT/KEY: keyed by (providerId, model), so a 2-account pool carries 2x the limit. A request that can't get a slot within concurrencyQueueTimeoutMs is treated as a backend 429 (→ account rotation → profile step). Editable at runtime (GET/POST /v1/concurrency) and persisted. See docs/failover.md#concurrency-limiting.
_config_shape.concurrencyQueueTimeoutMs: OPTIONAL number, default 45000. How long a request may wait in a full concurrency queue before it is treated as a backend 429. Must be >= 1000. Keep it under your client's request timeout so the safety valve fires first.
_config_shape.failoverRecoveryIntervalMs: OPTIONAL number, default 60000 — min ms between recovery probes (a profile winding back up / an account cooldown). Must be >= 1000.

_concurrency_doc: Per-(provider,model) simultaneous-request caps — the overflow is QUEUED, not degraded to a backup (see _config_shape.concurrency and docs/failover.md#concurrency-limiting). FIRST MATCH WINS so the specific tiers (glm-5.2=3, glm-5.1=6) are listed BEFORE the broad glm-*=8 catch-all; deepseek-v4-pro=200 is effectively unlimited (metered pay-as-you-go). PER ACCOUNT/KEY: on a route with an `accounts` pool the key is the account label, so a 2-account pool carries 2x the number here and a parked/exhausted key contributes 0. Remove this block for no concurrency limiting (unlimited).
_profiles_doc: A PROFILE is a named world of alias→target bindings. The client always sends a stable alias; the ACTIVE profile decides what it resolves to. EXAMPLE ABOVE: normally `native` (glm-5.2 → z.ai). `glm-on-sonnet` re-points glm-5.2 to your Anthropic reserve — it is BOTH the auto safety step AND hand-pinnable. `econ-opus` is manual-only (not in auto.steps): a cost combo you pin by hand. `budget` runs on a schedule during peak GLM-billing hours. Targets must resolve to a `routes` entry.
_auto_doc: auto.steps is the ordered automatic chain, best→fallback. steps[0] is the head/default (no `when`). Each later step carries `when` — the error class that steps DOWN to it: "limit" (429/529/quota/overload; default when omitted) or "5xx". A step-down only re-points aliases whose bind DIFFERS from the step above (an alias on a healthy independent provider is untouched). Priority of the active profile: manual pin > error-shift > open schedule window > defaultProfile. SAFETY IS ALWAYS ARMED: a failover error under a manual pin CLEARS the pin and the cascade proceeds. auto.recover:true winds the pointer back up once the higher step's backend recovers. auto.schedules set the intended profile during a wall-clock window (tz = fixed UTC offset like "+03:00"/"Z"; windows = ["HH:MM","HH:MM"] pairs, end exclusive, from>to wraps midnight).

routes._note: Anthropic route (your sonnet reserve): claude-* lands at api.anthropic.com with the client's own OAuth/subscription auth. The `glm-on-sonnet` profile rewrites glm-5.2 → claude-sonnet-5, which matches HERE. Change auth to { header: x-api-key, keyEnv: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY } (billing:metered) for a pay-as-you-go key.

GLM route with TWO z.ai Coding Plan subscriptions. accounts rotate zai-1 → zai-2 on a limit (BELOW profiles). Only when BOTH are drained does the auto chain step `native → glm-on-sonnet`, insuring glm-5.2 onto your paid Anthropic reserve. billing:subscription ⇒ the dashboard shows tokens + a link to the z.ai console, never a per-token $ estimate.
OpenRouter route for the `budget` profile (glm-5.2 → minimax/minimax-m3). Pay-as-you-go ⇒ billing defaults to metered; the dashboard shows real per-response $.
DeepSeek route: pay-as-you-go (metered). vision:false — DeepSeek soft-refuses images with a 200, so image-bearing requests are pre-routed to the forImages target without hitting it first.
Vision fallback target: when any backend rejects an image, the request is rerouted here with body.model rewritten to forImagesModel.
